facts.statement: Analog Devices is described in its SEC submissions as a semiconductor company, and its 10-K says it is a global semiconductor leader that bridges the physical and digital worlds to enable breakthroughs at the Intelligent Edge.
The proxy statement says ADI combines analog, digital, artificial intelligence (AI), and software technologies into solutions for customer challenges.
The proxy statement says ADI's solutions are aimed at advancements in automation and robotics, mobility, energy and data centers, and healthcare.

The board letter says ADI is placing AI in and around its products to enhance them and make them easier for customers to adopt and deploy, and is also leveraging AI within operations to improve productivity and operational efficiency.
For FY2025, SEC company facts show revenue of $12.306 billion.
For FY2025, SEC company facts show net income of $3.315 billion.
For FY2025, SEC company facts show operating cash flow of $4.818 billion.
For FY2025, SEC company facts show research and development expense of $1.660 billion.
For FY2025, SEC company facts show stockholders' equity of $35.176 billion.
For FY2025, SEC company facts show long-term debt of $6.634 billion.
For FY2025, SEC company facts show cash and cash equivalents of $1.991 billion.
ADI's common stock is listed on Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker ADI.
The 10-K states ADI's fiscal year ended November 1, 2025.
The 10-K states the aggregate market value of voting and non-voting common equity held by non-affiliates was approximately $81.121 billion based on the May 3, 2025 closing price.
The 10-K states there were 489,654,097 shares of common stock outstanding as of November 1, 2025.
Management concluded, and Ernst & Young attested, that ADI's internal control over financial reporting was effective as of November 1, 2025.
In the 2026 proxy appendix, ADI reported FY2025 gross margin of 61.5% and adjusted gross margin of 69.3%.
In the 2026 proxy appendix, ADI reported FY2025 operating margin of 26.6% and adjusted operating margin of 41.9%.
In the 2026 proxy appendix, ADI reported FY2025 free cash flow of $4.279 billion, equal to 39% of revenue.
The proxy appendix says free cash flow is used to repay debt obligations, make investments, fund acquisitions, and for certain other activities.
The 10-K lists a 2020 Equity Incentive Plan and an Amended & Restated 2022 Employee Stock Purchase Plan among its compensation-related exhibits.
The 10-K says ADI has adopted a written code of business conduct and ethics and an insider trading policy, both applying to relevant officers, directors, and employees.
The 10-K signature page identifies Vincent Roche as Chief Executive Officer and Chair of the Board of Directors.
The 10-K signature page identifies Richard C. Puccio, Jr. as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer.
The 10-K identifies Ernst & Young LLP, Boston, Massachusetts, as ADI's independent registered accounting firm.

The 2026 proxy states ADI's annual meeting is scheduled for March 11, 2026, and shareholders will vote on director elections, say-on-pay, auditor ratification, an amended and restated 2020 equity incentive plan, and a shareholder proposal.

The proxy's forward-looking statement section lists risks including tariffs and trade restrictions, semiconductor demand changes, supply chain disruptions, cybersecurity incidents, AI-related risks, litigation, indebtedness, and the ability to pay dividends or repurchase shares.
